## What this changes

<!-- One or two sentences. For an evidence change, name the source that drove it. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Prose: new or revised chapter content
- [ ] Evidence: new or corrected rows in `data/*.csv` and `references.bib`
- [ ] Generated tables regenerated from the data layer
- [ ] Tooling: scripts, schemas, workflows, or configuration
- [ ] Correction to previously merged work

## Evidence rules

<!-- Tick only what you have actually verified. An unticked box with an
     explanation is acceptable; a ticked box that is not true is not. -->

- [ ] Every numerical claim carries a footnote resolving to `data/sources.csv`
- [ ] Every claim about a current model carries an absolute date, not "currently" or "recently"
- [ ] Provider-reported figures are labelled Grade C at the point of use
- [ ] No generated region was edited by hand; tables were regenerated by their script
- [ ] Sources were read, not inferred; no benchmark result, price, specification, or citation was written from memory
- [ ] Where evidence is absent, the text says `Not publicly disclosed` or `Insufficient independent evidence`
- [ ] No benchmark scores are placed in the same ranked column under different or unstated evaluation conditions without an adjacent warning

## Quality control

<!-- The twelve-point checklist from research-methodology.md section 8. -->

- [ ] 1. Every numerical claim has a citation
- [ ] 2. Every current-model claim has a date
- [ ] 3. Provider claims are labelled
- [ ] 4. No incomparable settings compared without a warning
- [ ] 5. No argument duplicated from another chapter
- [ ] 6. No em dashes
- [ ] 7. Links resolve
- [ ] 8. Tables are consistent with their source CSV
- [ ] 9. Filenames match the MkDocs navigation
- [ ] 10. All validation scripts pass
- [ ] 11. `mkdocs build --strict` produces no warnings
- [ ] 12. The pages render correctly on GitHub

## Commands run

```
python scripts/validate_sources.py
python scripts/validate_tables.py
python scripts/validate_links.py
mkdocs build --strict
```

<!-- Paste the output if anything failed and you believe the failure is spurious. -->
